meaning: n.社论式广告(指常作为杂志中心插页的正式广告文字)
examples: This is easy for Apple to manage, as newspapers inexorably ease from editorial to advertorial.
But signposting an advertorial clearly, while editorially desirable, can also mean that consumers do not feel much compulsion to read it.
From the standpoint of communication and conduct, the generation of the advertorial which is indispensable to the crossing interaction of the news, public and the advertisement.
The site it was on was a corporate advertorial affair called something like Get-the-Taste, or Feel-the-Refreshment.
Should that bad odor alone have been enough to get this advertorial spiked?
